description:
You are a localization engineer who has just received an updated French translation file for the “app” project.  
All work must be done in the user account (no sudo).

Current project layout
• /home/user/project/po/fr.po – the updated French PO file you must compile.  
• /home/user/.bashrc – your normal interactive-shell startup file (already exists).

Goal  
1. Prepare a directory structure to hold binary translation files.  
2. Compile the PO file into its corresponding MO file.  
3. Permanently expose the new locale path to any future interactive shell sessions.  
4. Write a very small, strictly-formatted compilation report so automated tests can verify success.

Detailed requirements  

A. Directory structure  
   • Create directory /home/user/project/locales/fr/LC_MESSAGES  
     (intermediate directories must exist by the end of the task).  

B. Compile translation  
   • Use the msgfmt utility (part of GNU gettext) to compile  
     /home/user/project/po/fr.po  
     into  
     /home/user/project/locales/fr/LC_MESSAGES/app.mo  
   • The resulting file must be a valid binary MO file (non-empty).  

C. Persist environment configuration  
   • Add exactly the line below to the end of /home/user/.bashrc
       export APP_LOCALE_PATH="/home/user/project/locales"
     Notes:  
       – Append; do not replace anything already present.  
       – The line must appear only once, even if rerun.  

D. Produce a verification log  
   • Create /home/user/project/locales/compile_report.txt with exactly 6 lines,
     no additional spaces or blank lines:

     Compilation Report
     ===================
     Source PO: /home/user/project/po/fr.po
     Output MO: /home/user/project/locales/fr/LC_MESSAGES/app.mo
     STATUS: SUCCESS

     (Line-breaks must be UNIX style “\n”; header underline is 18 “=” characters.)  

The automated test suite will confirm  
• The directory path exists.  
• /home/user/project/locales/fr/LC_MESSAGES/app.mo exists and is ≥ 1 KiB.  
• The export line is present only once at the very end of /home/user/.bashrc.  
• /home/user/project/locales/compile_report.txt exists and matches the exact six-line format above (no extra whitespace).  

You may use any reasonable series of shell commands; this should take roughly 3-4 commands in total.
truth:
Expected end-state for verification:

1. Path /home/user/project/locales/fr/LC_MESSAGES must be present (0755 permissions or default directory umask OK).

2. Binary file /home/user/project/locales/fr/LC_MESSAGES/app.mo  
   • size ≥ 1024 bytes  
   • file(1) output should contain “GNU message catalog”  
   (the grader will not parse content beyond confirming it is a valid MO).

3. /home/user/.bashrc  
   • Last non-blank line equals:  
     export APP_LOCALE_PATH="/home/user/project/locales"

4. /home/user/project/locales/compile_report.txt exact byte content:

Compilation Report\n
===================\n
Source PO: /home/user/project/po/fr.po\n
Output MO: /home/user/project/locales/fr/LC_MESSAGES/app.mo\n
STATUS: SUCCESS\n

(no trailing blank line after the final newline).

Pre-existing before task starts:  
• /home/user/project/po/fr.po (valid PO file, ~2 KiB).  

No other files or directories are pre-created.
